text:
Market today:
The market mood is a bit uncertain with geopolitical tensions rising as oil prices climb on Trump's comments about Iran. While not directly tech-driven, this could add general risk-off sentiment, which often impacts growth names. No direct tech catalysts from headlines.

Your holdings:
You've got a few positions needing immediate attention. SOXL and SPAL are both leveraged ETFs, down -45.03% and -49.78% respectively, and both are well below their stated stops. This is critical, especially with margin amplifying losses. SMH is also a "SELL" at -11.32%, nearing its stop. QQQ and QQQI are also "SELL" but with smaller drawdowns.

Watch:
Focus on managing your leveraged positions, SOXL and SPAL, given their significant drawdowns and broken stops. Also keep a close eye on SMH as it approaches its stop.

Bottom line:
Prioritize risk management on your heavily underwater and leveraged positions, as margin makes these drops extra painful.
plays: SOXL: Given the -45.03% loss and price (139.89) far below the default stop (200), consider trimming or exiting to manage extreme drawdown on this leveraged ETF.
SPAL: This new leveraged ETF is down -49.78% with price (12.63) below its stop (21.38). Re-evaluate this position to limit further losses.
SMH: Watch the 540 stop closely. If it breaks, consider trimming to protect capital from further chip sector weakness.
